ImageDescription.value:
Collage of 24 famous Serbs.
  • The first row, left to right:
    Jovan Vladimir, medieval ruler and saint;
    Stefan Nemanja, medieval ruler, founder of Nemanjić dynasty;
    Saint Sava, patron saint, the first Archbishop of the autocephalous Serbian Church, diplomat, writer;
    Prince Lazar, medieval ruler;
    Tzar Dušan the Mighty, the most powerful medieval ruler, Emperor of the Serbs and (nominally) the Greeks;
    Sokollu Mehmed Pasha - Mehmed-paša Sokolović, Ottoman statesman;
  • The second row, left to right:
    Zaharije Orfelin, polymath;
    Dositej Obradović, author, philosopher, traveler and the first minister of education of Serbia;
    Karađorđe, founder of modern Serbia and revolutionary;
    Vuk Stefanović Karadžić, philologist, linguist, father of the study of Serbian folklore;
    Petar Petrović Njegoš, poet, writer, prince-bishop of Montenegro;
    Nikola Pašić, politician and statesman;
  • The third row, left to right:
    Živojin Mišić, Vojvoda and military commander;
    Stevan Stojanović Mokranjac, composer and music educator;
    Nikola Tesla, inventor, electrical engineer, mechanical engineer, physicist, and futurist;
    Mihajlo Idvorski Pupin, physicist and physical chemist;
    Paja Jovanović, realist painter.;
    Jovan Cvijić, geographer;
  • The fourth row, left to right:
    Nadežda Petrović, fauvist painter;
    Jovan Dučić, poet, writer and diplomat;
    Marina Abramović, performance artist;
    Emir Kusturica, filmmaker, actor and musician;
    Vlade Divac, basketball player;
    Novak Đoković, World No. 1 professional tennis player;
